channel.title: Ilia Klishin Publications Feed
channel.description: Publication cards with concise summaries and links to original sources.

item.title: Putin-Macron talks and the Russia expertise gap
item.description:
In this Feb 14, 2022 Moscow Times opinion piece, Ilya Klishin argues that a small factual error in foreign reporting around Putin-Macron talks exposed a larger expertise problem in commentary about Russia. The trigger is a repeated claim that Putin quoted a specific punk song after his meeting with Emmanuel Macron.
It connects media analysis with the original text published by The Moscow Times.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2022/02/14/putin-macron-talks-highlight-gap-in-russia-expertise-a76375

item.title: Mass protests: lessons from a decade
item.description:
This essay reevaluates the 2011 Bolotnaya protests not as a tactical failure, but as a structural inevitability. Framing the defeat as a Hegelian catalyst, it argues the subsequent crackdown inadvertently served as a "civil society university," transforming a naive public into a deeply politicized society primed for an unpredictable historical synthesis.
The summary gives a clear reference point for later comparisons.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/12/10/russias-mass-protests-10-years-on-finding-hope-in-apparent-defeat-a75781

item.title: Commentary on pressure against independent media institutions
item.description:
This card summarizes "Commentary on pressure against independent media institutions" published by The Moscow Times on 2021-08-24.
This piece places media freedom in a concrete editorial moment in 2021.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/08/24/the-kremlin-is-coming-for-media-one-by-one-and-society-is-helpless-to-stop-it-a74879

item.title: The Magic Mirror of Russian Public Opinion
item.description:
How public-opinion narratives are shaped by institutions, incentives, and media distribution logic. This card summarizes "The Magic Mirror of Russian Public Opinion" published by The Moscow Times on 2021-07-12.
It connects public opinion with the original text published by The Moscow Times.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/07/12/the-magic-mirror-of-public-opinion-a74492

item.title: The Dilemma of the Great Terror: How to Report Roman Protasevich's Confession?
item.description:
A short summary of media ethics, with a direct link to the original publication.
The summary gives a clear reference point for later comparisons.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/06/07/the-dilemma-of-the-great-terror-how-to-report-roman-protasevichs-confession-a74125

item.title: Let's Wait Until After the Elections
item.description:
How election-timing rhetoric can postpone decisions and normalize strategic waiting. This card summarizes "Let's Wait Until After the Elections" published by The Moscow Times on 2021-05-17.
It explains electoral timing and links directly to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/05/17/lets-wait-until-after-the-elections-a73915

item.title: Luiza Rozova and the Helpless Intellectual
item.description:
The piece focuses on elite discourse and explains why the issue mattered in public debate in 2021.
This piece places elite discourse in a concrete editorial moment in 2021.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/03/19/luiza-rozova-and-the-helpless-intellectual-a73301

item.title: Bloomberg quotes Klishin on Russia’s Twitter crackdown
item.description:
This Bloomberg report covers Russia’s move to slow down Twitter as part of a broader crackdown on Western social platforms. Klishin is not the author, but he is quoted in the piece as it explains how the authorities were tightening pressure while still stopping short of a full block.
External feature card documenting how Bloomberg used Klishin commentary while reporting Russia’s platform pressure strategy.
Original source: https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-03-10/russia-takes-aim-at-twitter-over-content-in-unprecedented-crackdown

item.title: Four Ways the Kremlin Could Block Western Social Networks in Russia
item.description:
Which technical and political mechanisms can be used to restrict social-network access. This card summarizes "Four Ways the Kremlin Could Block Western Social Networks in Russia" published by The Moscow Times on 2021-02-12.
It connects social network regulation with the original text published by The Moscow Times.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/02/12/four-ways-the-kremlin-could-block-western-social-networks-in-russia-a72920

item.title: The Capitol Storming Gives Russians an Escape From Their Reality
item.description:
This The Moscow Times (2021) publication examines comparative media framing and summarizes the main argument.
The summary gives a clear reference point for later comparisons.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2021/01/14/the-capitol-storming-gives-russians-an-escape-from-their-reality-a72598

item.title: Warning! An Army of Russian Trolls Has Invaded TikTok
item.description:
How platform dynamics and troll methods move from domestic messaging into wider information spaces. This card summarizes "Warning!
It explains disinformation and platforms and links directly to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/12/18/warning-an-army-of-russian-trolls-has-invaded-tiktok-a72410

item.title: Why Russians Dislike Good Russians in American Films No Less Than Bad Russians
item.description:
The article outlines cultural representation in clear terms and places it in editorial context.
It connects cultural representation with the original text published by The Moscow Times.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/11/20/why-russians-dislike-good-russians-in-american-films-no-less-than-bad-russians-a72097

item.title: Great Russian Literature Versus One Tweet by Stephen King
item.description:
How cultural symbols and representation frames affect trust, identity, and audience reaction. This card summarizes "Great Russian Literature Versus One Tweet by Stephen King" published by The Moscow Times on 2020-11-09.
The summary gives a clear reference point for later comparisons.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/11/09/great-russian-literature-versus-one-tweet-by-stephen-king-a71993

item.title: Anonymous Telegram Channels Are Shaping Russia's Daily Life
item.description:
The piece focuses on platform influence and explains why the issue mattered in public debate in 2020.
It explains platform influence and links directly to the primary source.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/09/28/anonymous-telegram-channels-are-shaping-russias-reality-a71588

item.title: Why Do Russians Resent Western Media Over The
item.description:
Why protest outcomes that look like defeat can still produce long-term civic learning. This card summarizes "Belarus Coverage Framing: The Moscow Times (2020-08-14) - Why Do Russians Resent Western Media Over The" published by The Moscow Times on 2020-08-14.
This piece places belarus coverage framing in a concrete editorial moment in 2020.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/08/14/why-do-russians-resent-western-media-over-the-protests-in-belarus-a71155

item.title: Coronavirus Spoils Putin's 'Forever-in-Power' Public Vote
item.description:
The column explains how the pandemic disrupted a carefully staged constitutional plebiscite. Klishin argues that the Kremlin lost timing control and faced a strategic bind where postponement and continuation both carried rising political costs.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/04/16/coronavirus-spoils-putins-forever-in-power-vote-a70014

item.title: Le coronavirus deraille le vote public de Poutine pour rester au pouvoir
item.description:
La chronique explique comment la pandemie a perturbe un plebiscite constitutionnel soigneusement mis en scene. Klishin soutient que le Kremlin a perdu le controle du calendrier et s est retrouve dans un dilemme ou report et poursuite augmentaient les couts politiques.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/04/16/coronavirus-spoils-putins-forever-in-power-vote-a70014

item.title: Coronavirus durchkreuzt Putins Abstimmung fuer ewige Macht
item.description:
Die Kolumne zeigt, wie die Pandemie ein sorgfaeltig inszeniertes Verfassungsplebiszit stoerte. Klishin argumentiert, dass der Kreml die Zeitkontrolle verlor und in ein strategisches Dilemma geriet, in dem Aufschub wie Fortsetzung steigende politische Kosten erzeugten.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/04/16/coronavirus-spoils-putins-forever-in-power-vote-a70014

item.title: El coronavirus arruina la votacion publica de Putin para perpetuarse en el poder
item.description:
La columna explica como la pandemia altero un plebiscito constitucional cuidadosamente escenificado. Klishin sostiene que el Kremlin perdio el control del calendario y quedo en un dilema estrategico donde aplazar o continuar elevaba los costos politicos.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/04/16/coronavirus-spoils-putins-forever-in-power-vote-a70014

item.title: Why Medvedev's resignation and Putin's amendments changed nothing
item.description:
This Russian-language Moscow Times column argues that both events were staged inside a fixed power design. Klishin presents personnel rotation and constitutional theater as symbolic moves that changed optics but did not alter the real center of decision-making.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/01/17/10-2-a68947

item.title: Pourquoi la demission de Medvedev et les amendements de Poutine n'ont rien change
item.description:
Cette chronique russophone du Moscow Times soutient que les deux evenements se deroulent dans un schema de pouvoir deja verrouille. Klishin decrit la rotation des postes et le theatre constitutionnel comme des gestes symboliques qui changent l image, pas le centre des decisions.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/01/17/10-2-a68947

item.title: Warum Medwedews Ruecktritt und Putins Verfassungsaenderungen nichts aenderten
item.description:
Diese russischsprachige Kolumne der Moscow Times argumentiert, dass beide Ereignisse in ein bereits festes Machtschema passen. Klishin beschreibt Personalwechsel und Verfassungstheater als symbolische Schritte, die die Optik aendern, aber nicht das eigentliche Entscheidungszentrum.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/01/17/10-2-a68947

item.title: Por que la renuncia de Medvedev y las enmiendas de Putin no cambiaron nada
item.description:
Esta columna en ruso de The Moscow Times sostiene que ambos hechos ocurrieron dentro de un diseno de poder ya cerrado. Klishin describe la rotacion de cargos y el teatro constitucional como movimientos simbolicos que cambian la imagen, pero no el centro real de decision.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2020/01/17/10-2-a68947

item.title: Who owns Russian? And who owns English?
item.description:
The column examines how language is politicized as an instrument of cultural control. Klishin compares claims over Russian and English to show that language policy often masks power struggles over identity, legitimacy, and who may define public norms.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/11/11/9-a68123

item.title: A qui appartient le russe? Et a qui appartient l anglais?
item.description:
La chronique examine la politisation de la langue comme instrument de controle culturel. Klishin compare les pretentions sur le russe et l anglais pour montrer que la politique linguistique masque souvent des luttes de pouvoir sur l identite, la legitimite et la norme publique.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/11/11/9-a68123

item.title: Wem gehoert Russisch? Und wem gehoert Englisch?
item.description:
Die Kolumne untersucht, wie Sprache als Instrument kultureller Kontrolle politisiert wird. Klishin vergleicht Ansprueche auf Russisch und Englisch und zeigt, dass Sprachpolitik oft Machtkaempfe um Identitaet, Legitimation und oeffentliche Normen verdeckt.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/11/11/9-a68123

item.title: A quien pertenece el ruso? Y a quien pertenece el ingles?
item.description:
La columna analiza como el lenguaje se politiza como herramienta de control cultural. Klishin compara las reclamaciones sobre ruso e ingles para mostrar que la politica linguistica suele encubrir luchas de poder por identidad, legitimidad y normas publicas.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/11/11/9-a68123

item.title: Why shoot Navalny if you can bankrupt him
item.description:
This Russian-language column frames financial pressure as a central tool of modern repression. Instead of visible violence, authorities can exhaust opponents through fines, frozen accounts, and legal costs while preserving the appearance of procedural legality.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/klishin-why-hold-mass-executions-when-bankruptcies-work-a67972

item.title: Pourquoi fusiller Navalny si on peut le ruiner
item.description:
Cette chronique en russe presente la pression financiere comme outil central de repression moderne. Au lieu d une violence visible, les autorites peuvent epuiser les opposants par amendes, gels de comptes et frais juridiques tout en conservant une apparence de legalite procedurale.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/klishin-why-hold-mass-executions-when-bankruptcies-work-a67972

item.title: Warum Navalny erschiessen, wenn man ihn ruinieren kann
item.description:
Diese russischsprachige Kolumne beschreibt finanziellen Druck als zentrales Werkzeug moderner Repression. Statt sichtbarer Gewalt koennen Behoerden Gegner durch Strafen, Kontensperren und Rechtskosten erschoepfen und dabei den Schein formaler Legalitaet wahren.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/klishin-why-hold-mass-executions-when-bankruptcies-work-a67972

item.title: Por que fusilar a Navalny si se le puede arruinar
item.description:
Esta columna en ruso presenta la presion financiera como herramienta central de represion moderna. En lugar de violencia visible, las autoridades pueden agotar a los opositores con multas, bloqueo de cuentas y costos legales mientras mantienen una apariencia de legalidad.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/klishin-why-hold-mass-executions-when-bankruptcies-work-a67972

item.title: How Putin Is Bankrupting the Russian Opposition
item.description:
This English column describes bankruptcy tactics as a calibrated instrument of repression. Instead of spectacular crackdowns, the state can deplete opposition capacity through legal debts, penalties, and procedural pressure while preserving formal plausibility.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/how-putin-is-bankrupting-the-russian-opposition-a67971

item.title: Comment Poutine ruine l opposition russe
item.description:
Cette chronique en anglais decrit la faillite forcee comme instrument de repression calibree. Au lieu de grandes rafles spectaculaires, l Etat peut epuiser l opposition par dettes juridiques, sanctions et pression procedurale tout en conservant une plausibilite formelle.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/how-putin-is-bankrupting-the-russian-opposition-a67971

item.title: Wie Putin die russische Opposition in den Bankrott treibt
item.description:
Die englische Kolumne beschreibt erzwungene Insolvenz als kalibriertes Repressionsinstrument. Statt spektakulaerer Razzien kann der Staat oppositionelle Kapazitaet durch Rechtskosten, Strafen und Verfahrensdruck erschoepfen und zugleich formale Plausibilitaet bewahren.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/how-putin-is-bankrupting-the-russian-opposition-a67971

item.title: Como Putin lleva a la quiebra a la oposicion rusa
item.description:
Esta columna en ingles describe la quiebra forzada como instrumento calibrado de represion. En lugar de redadas espectaculares, el Estado puede agotar la capacidad opositora mediante deudas legales, sanciones y presion procesal, manteniendo una apariencia formal.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/30/how-putin-is-bankrupting-the-russian-opposition-a67971

item.title: How the death penalty debate linked to a ban on negative Ukraine coverage
item.description:
Klishin connects two media agendas to show how distraction campaigns are built. The text argues that emotionally loaded topics can be amplified to redirect public attention and normalize selective information boundaries around politically sensitive narratives.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/16/8-2-a67761

item.title: Comment le debat sur la peine de mort a ete lie a l interdiction du negatif sur l Ukraine
item.description:
Klishin relie deux agendas mediatiques pour montrer comment se construisent les campagnes de diversion. Le texte explique que des sujets tres emotionnels peuvent etre amplifies pour deplacer l attention publique et normaliser des frontieres selectives de l information.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/16/8-2-a67761

item.title: Wie die Debatte ueber die Todesstrafe mit dem Verbot negativer Ukraine-Berichte verknuepft wurde
item.description:
Klishin verknuepft zwei Medienagenden und zeigt, wie Ablenkungskampagnen konstruiert werden. Der Text argumentiert, dass emotional aufgeladene Themen verstaerkt werden koennen, um Aufmerksamkeit umzuleiten und selektive Informationsgrenzen bei sensiblen Narrativen zu normalisieren.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/16/8-2-a67761

item.title: Como el debate sobre la pena de muerte se vinculo con la prohibicion de cobertura negativa sobre Ucrania
item.description:
Klishin conecta dos agendas mediaticas para mostrar como se construyen campanas de distraccion. El texto sostiene que temas con alta carga emocional pueden amplificarse para desviar la atencion publica y normalizar limites selectivos de informacion.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/10/16/8-2-a67761

item.title: Russias New Heroes Are Those Who Simply Say Things as They Are
item.description:
Klishin analyzes why plain factual speech became politically meaningful in a distorted information environment. The piece argues that when institutions fail, even minimal honesty from insiders can shift public attention and recalibrate legitimacy.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/09/26/russias-new-heroes-are-those-who-simply-say-things-as-they-are-a67400

item.title: Les nouveaux heros de la Russie sont ceux qui disent simplement les choses
item.description:
Klishin analyse pourquoi la parole factuelle est devenue politiquement significative dans un environnement informationnel deforme. Le texte soutient que, lorsque les institutions echouent, une honnetete minimale d acteurs internes peut deplacer l attention publique et recalibrer la legitimite.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/09/26/russias-new-heroes-are-those-who-simply-say-things-as-they-are-a67400

item.title: Russlands neue Helden sind jene, die Dinge einfach aussprechen
item.description:
Klishin analysiert, warum schlichte Faktensprache in einem verzerrten Informationsumfeld politisch bedeutsam wurde. Der Text argumentiert, dass bei institutionellem Versagen selbst minimale Ehrlichkeit von Insidern oeffentliche Aufmerksamkeit verschieben und Legitimation neu justieren kann.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/09/26/russias-new-heroes-are-those-who-simply-say-things-as-they-are-a67400

item.title: Los nuevos heroes de Rusia son quienes simplemente dicen las cosas como son
item.description:
Klishin analiza por que el habla factual simple se volvio politicamente significativa en un entorno informativo distorsionado. El texto sostiene que, cuando las instituciones fallan, incluso una honestidad minima de actores internos puede mover la atencion publica y recalibrar legitimidad.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/09/26/russias-new-heroes-are-those-who-simply-say-things-as-they-are-a67400

item.title: Why Russian Journalists Find Refuge in Propaganda Outlets
item.description:
The article examines structural reasons why independent journalists move into propaganda institutions. Klishin argues that contraction of the independent market and career precarity create conditions where ideological compromise becomes a professional survival strategy.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/04/05/why-russian-journalists-find-refuge-in-propaganda-outlets-a65108

item.title: Pourquoi des journalistes russes trouvent refuge dans des medias de propagande
item.description:
L article examine les raisons structurelles qui poussent des journalistes independants vers des institutions de propagande. Klishin soutient que la contraction du marche independant et la precarite des carrieres creent des conditions ou le compromis ideologique devient une strategie de survie.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/04/05/why-russian-journalists-find-refuge-in-propaganda-outlets-a65108

item.title: Warum russische Journalisten Zuflucht in Propaganda-Medien suchen
item.description:
Der Artikel untersucht strukturelle Gruende, warum unabhaengige Journalisten in Propaganda-Institutionen wechseln. Klishin argumentiert, dass Schrumpfung des unabhaengigen Marktes und berufliche Prekaritaet Bedingungen schaffen, unter denen ideologischer Kompromiss zur Ueberlebensstrategie wird.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/04/05/why-russian-journalists-find-refuge-in-propaganda-outlets-a65108

item.title: Por que periodistas rusos encuentran refugio en medios de propaganda
item.description:
El articulo examina razones estructurales por las que periodistas independientes pasan a instituciones de propaganda. Klishin sostiene que la contraccion del mercado independiente y la precariedad profesional crean condiciones donde el compromiso ideologico se vuelve estrategia de supervivencia.
Entrada agregada para incluir esta publicacion en la version en espanol con fuente verificada.
Original source: https://www.themoscowtimes.com/2019/04/05/why-russian-journalists-find-refuge-in-propaganda-outlets-a65108

item.title: The music of the media desert
item.description:
This Vedomosti column describes how shrinking pluralism produces a repetitive and hostile media environment. Klishin argues that the system rewards noise and intimidation, while public discussion loses nuance, memory, and professional standards.
Entry added to include this publication in the English corpus with a verified source link.
Original source: https://www.vedomosti.ru/opinion/columns/2018/12/06/788550-muzika-mediinoi-pustini

item.title: La musique du desert mediatique
item.description:
Cette chronique de Vedomosti decrit comment la reduction du pluralisme cree un environnement mediatique repetitif et hostile. Klishin soutient que le systeme recompense le bruit et l intimidation, tandis que le debat public perd nuance, memoire et standards professionnels.
Entree ajoutee pour couvrir cette publication dans la version francaise avec source verifiee.
Original source: https://www.vedomosti.ru/opinion/columns/2018/12/06/788550-muzika-mediinoi-pustini

item.title: Die Musik der medialen Wueste
item.description:
Diese Vedomosti-Kolumne beschreibt, wie schwindender Pluralismus eine repetitive und feindliche Medienumgebung erzeugt. Klishin argumentiert, dass das System Larm und Einschuechterung belohnt, waehrend der oeffentliche Diskurs Nuance, Erinnerung und professionelle Standards verliert.
Eintrag ergaenzt, damit diese Publikation in der deutschen Version mit verifizierter Quelle enthalten ist.
Original source: https://www.vedomosti.ru/opinion/columns/2018/12/06/788550-muzika-mediinoi-pustini
